file_insights.changes_summary: Integrated StateManager for persistent state management, replacing direct JSON file I/O in set_active_trade()
key_modifications: Initialized StateManager in init_symbol() with per-symbol namespace and state file path
Replaced json.dump() with state_manager.set_active_trade() method call

patterns_discovered.description: Direct file I/O operations abstracted into StateManager API methods, improving separation of concerns
StateManager initialized with namespace=f'scanner_{s}' to isolate state per symbol
state_manager global variable declared and initialized in init_symbol(), called per-symbol
Original try-except blocks maintained during migration to ensure backward compatibility

gotchas_discovered.impact: state_manager is re-initialized each time init_symbol() is called, potentially losing state across symbol switches
bare 'except Exception: pass' blocks hide StateManager method failures silently
Code assumes StateManager.get_trade_state() and StateManager.set_active_trade() exist and return compatible data structures

discoveries.approach_summary: Successfully migrated set_active_trade() from direct JSON file operations to StateManager abstraction. Integration is clean with proper global variable management and per-symbol namespace isolation. Error handling patterns preserved for backward compatibility.

recommendations.recommendation: Implement caching or lazy-loading strategy for StateManager to avoid re-initialization on each symbol switch
Add explicit logging around StateManager method calls to diagnose state issues in production
Verify StateManager API contract (get_trade_state return type, set_active_trade signature) matches current usage
Replace bare except blocks with specific exception handling for StateManager operations
Add type hints and docstrings documenting StateManager interface expectations
